description_long: This sculpture depicts a surreal, headless humanoid figure with four elongated arms extending outward from its torso, two on each side. The body is anatomically detailed, with visible musculature and a mottled, grayish skin texture that gives it an unsettling, almost corpse-like appearance. From the lower abdomen and groin area, several long, thin, tentacle-like appendages protrude, adding to its otherworldly and disturbing presence. The figure stands upright on two legs, with hands and feet rendered in a realistic yet exaggerated manner. The surface appears rough and matte, with subtle variations in color suggesting bruising or decay. The overall form is both human and monstrous, evoking a sense of unease and fascination.
description: A headless, four-armed humanoid sculpture with tentacle-like appendages emerging from its lower torso, featuring a mottled, grayish surface and detailed anatomy. The figure stands upright, blending human and monstrous elements.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back musculature, four arms extended outward, and the absence of a head, with legs close together.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the torso, the length of the arms, and the protruding tentacle-like appendages from the lower abdomen.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the chest, four arms, and the full extent of the tentacle-like appendages emerging from the groin area.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the side profile and the splayed tentacle appendages.
attribute_subject_matter: surreal headless humanoid with four arms and tentacle appendages
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is plain and featureless.
